findings.description: Direct contradiction on execution topology: Overview states all pipeline stages run in parallel while Stage 2 states Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.
findings.evidence: Overview: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' Stage 2: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'

findings.description: Batch/streaming performance claim is factually inverted. Streaming outperforms batch for small files; batch incurs setup overhead that provides no benefit at small sizes.
findings.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' Standard data engineering: streaming outperforms batch at small sizes due to batch setup overhead.

findings.description: 99.9% latency reduction claim is implausible without supporting data — no baseline, conditions, or methodology provided.
findings.evidence: 'The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes.' No baseline, conditions, or methodology cited.

findings.description: Dangling reference to non-existent Section 6 for security documentation. Document ends at Conclusions with no Section 6.
findings.evidence: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' Document ends at Conclusions with no Section 6 present.

findings.description: Benchmark claim unattributed — 'Benchmarks show' with no source, methodology, or conditions named.
findings.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' No source or methodology cited.

findings.description: 'Pipeline' and 'workflow' used interchangeably and inconsistently throughout — treated as distinct in Operational Notes and as distinct-but-different in Conclusions, with no definitions provided.
findings.evidence: Operational Notes: 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored.' Conclusions: 'a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.' No definitions provided.

findings.description: 'Standard retry policy' referenced but never defined — no retry count, backoff, or timeout specified.
findings.evidence: 'Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures.' No definition of retry count, backoff, or timeout anywhere in the document.

findings.location: Stage 4 — Monitoring paragraph 1; Stage 3 — Delivery paragraph 1
findings.description: 5-minute polling interval in Stage 4 contradicts the sub-second latency implication of the 99.9% reduction claim in Stage 3 — these two statements are internally inconsistent.
findings.evidence: Stage 4: 'The monitoring system polls health endpoints every 5 minutes.' Stage 3: 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9%' (implying sub-second delivery). 5-minute polling cannot detect or support sub-second latency SLOs.

findings.description: Conclusions describe adding real-time streaming as a future enhancement, but the document already describes the system as handling both batch and streaming — scope contradiction.
findings.evidence: 'Future enhancements include adding a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.' Stage 2 already discusses both batch and streaming processing modes.

findings.description: ETL acronym not expanded on first use.
findings.evidence: 'an internal ETL system' — ETL not expanded on first use.

findings.description: 'Large files' handled differently via overflow queue but no size threshold is defined.
findings.evidence: 'Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.' No size threshold specified.

findings.description: Stage 4 Monitoring section and Operational Notes repeat substantially the same information about the Prometheus dashboard — near-duplicate content.
findings.evidence: Stage 4: 'The monitoring system uses a Prometheus dashboard.' Operational Notes bullet 1: 'Monitored via the Prometheus dashboard.' Near-duplicate.
